title: ferray-numpy-interop: bool/non-bool split requires separate trait names (ToArrow vs ToArrowBool)
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Arrow and Polars each have two separate trait pairs: ToArrow/FromArrow for numeric types and ToArrowBool/FromArrowBool for booleans. This is because Arrow/Polars use bit-packed storage for booleans (different type than PrimitiveArray). The result is that users must remember to call .to_arrow() for numerics and the bool-specific trait method for booleans. Consider a unified trait with an associated output type that dispatches correctly, or at minimum document the split prominently.
